@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: e280
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'FocusScope: centralize the focus decoration — ONE canonical visual driven by Rust focus state'
+---
+## What
+
+Today every consumer of `FocusScope` with `renderContainer={false}` (row selector in `data-table.tsx`, view buttons in `left-nav.tsx`, perspective tabs in `perspective-tab-bar.tsx`, fixture cells in `spatial-grid-fixture.tsx`) has to re-implement the same three-step dance to paint a focus ring:
+
+1. Call `useFocusedMoniker()` from `@/lib/entity-focus-context`
+2. Compare it against the scope's moniker to derive a local `isFocused` boolean
+3. Set `data-focused={isFocused || undefined}` + merge a `ring-*` class into the element's `className`
+
+That duplicates React state that Rust already owns (the focused spatial key, mirrored into React via the `focus-changed` event listener and `useSyncExternalStore`). The three pending visual-fix tasks (`01KPQX6TEZG9SG88B31KGKS2D5`, `01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP`, `01KPQY69TH76VN330BH2J24YSX`) are all variants of the same defect and would each install their own copy of this pattern. One canonical decoration — owned by `FocusScope` itself — collapses all three.
+
+### Why the duplication exists
+
+`FocusHighlight` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/ui/focus-highlight.tsx`) is the intended single visual primitive: it renders a `<div>` with `data-focused={focused || undefined}` and scroll-into-view behavior. `FocusScope` uses it only when `renderContainer={true}`. When `renderContainer={false}` (unavoidable for `<td>`, `<tr>`, a Radix `TooltipTrigger asChild`-forwarded `<button>`), `FocusScope` renders nothing of its own — so no `FocusHighlight` mounts and the visual disappears. Consumers patch this per-site.
+
+### The architectural fix
+
+Make `FocusScope` imperatively set `data-focused="true"` on the scope's attached DOM element whenever the scope is claimed, regardless of `renderContainer`. The scope already has `elementRef` for rect measurement (both modes: `renderContainer={true}` attaches via `FocusHighlight`'s internal ref, `renderContainer={false}` attaches via `useFocusScopeElementRef()` in the consumer). Reuse that ref — in a `useEffect` gated on `isClaimed`, write/clear the attribute on `elementRef.current`. The DOM node owns exactly one state signal, React doesn't mirror it into a local `useState`, and no consumer has to re-implement it.
+
+Pair the attribute with a single global CSS rule that paints the ring:
+
+```css
+/* kanban-app/ui/src/index.css */
+[data-focused] {
+  @apply ring-2 ring-primary ring-inset;
+  /* ring-offset-* added selectively via :where() or per-surface CSS vars if needed */
+}
+```
+
+Consumers that want to opt out of the default visual (e.g. the quick-capture overlay in `store-container.tsx` uses `showFocusBar={false}` today) continue to pass `showFocusBar={false}`; when set, `FocusScope` skips the attribute write. `showFocusBar`'s existing API stays but its plumbing goes through one place instead of a prop drilled into `FocusHighlight`.
+
+`FocusHighlight` collapses: its only unique behavior beyond `data-focused` is `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` on focus. Move that effect into `FocusScope` (gated on the same `isClaimed`-became-true transition). Delete `FocusHighlight`, or keep it as a thin re-export if any non-`FocusScope` caller relies on it (verify via grep).
+
+### What each consumer gains (all automatic once this lands)
+
+- `data-table.tsx` — `RowSelectorTd` and `DataTableCellTd` drop any planned `useFocusedMoniker()` + `data-focused` wiring (the row-selector visual bug `01KPQX6TEZG9SG88B31KGKS2D5` resolves as a no-op fix).
+- `left-nav.tsx` — `ViewButtonElement` drops the planned manual subscription; ring shows when the button is focused. Unrelated Enter-to-activate handling in `01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP` remains its own task.
+- `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` — `PerspectiveTab` drops the planned manual subscription; `01KPQY69TH76VN330BH2J24YSX` resolves as a no-op fix.
+- `spatial-grid-fixture.tsx` — `FixtureCellDiv` can drop its `useFocusedMoniker()` + `data-focused` lines. The attribute shows up on the same `<div>` via `FocusScope` automatically.
+
+Those three visual-fix tasks can be closed as superseded after this lands — note in each when committing.
+
+### Files touched
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` — `useSpatialClaim` or a new `useFocusAttribute` hook imperatively writes/clears `data-focused` on `elementRef.current` gated by `isClaimed && showFocusBar`. Move `scrollIntoView` from `FocusHighlight` here.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/ui/focus-highlight.tsx` — delete the `data-focused` + scroll plumbing; either collapse the component into a simple `<div>`/`<section>` wrapper that exists only to provide a container (so `renderContainer={true}` still has a DOM node to attach the ref to), or inline that rendering into `FocusScope` and remove the file entirely.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/index.css` (or whatever global stylesheet the project uses — verify via `grep -r "@tailwind"`) — add one `[data-focused]` rule with the ring utility classes.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-grid-fixture.tsx` — drop the manual `data-focused={isFocused || undefined}` on `FixtureCellDiv`; assertions in the test files keep working because the attribute now comes from `FocusScope`.
+
+Tests touched (assertions do NOT need to change — the attribute still lands on the same DOM node, just via a different mechanism):
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.test.tsx` — add explicit coverage for `renderContainer={false}` scopes getting `data-focused` via the ref write path (currently 0 coverage for that case because the per-consumer pattern was the workaround).
+- Run the full `spatial-nav-*.test.tsx` suite to confirm no regression.
+
+### Out of scope
+
+- Enter-to-activate on LeftNav buttons (`01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP`) — that's a keybinding concern, not a visual one.
+- Card sub-parts / `parent_scope` routing (`01KPNWP1KA`) — independent feature.
+- `data-active` semantics — remains the right attribute for "this view/perspective is currently selected" (distinct from "this scope is spatially focused"). Not removing it.
+- Non-entity scopes using `FocusHighlight` directly (e.g. `column-view.tsx` column headers) — audit, but changing them is not required by this task.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `FocusScope` sets `data-focused="true"` on its attached element when the scope is claimed, for BOTH `renderContainer={true}` and `renderContainer={false}` scopes
+- [ ] `FocusScope` clears `data-focused` when the scope is unclaimed (same element)
+- [ ] `showFocusBar={false}` suppresses the attribute write entirely (preserves quick-capture / non-decorated scope use cases)
+- [ ] `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` fires on claim for all scopes that opt in via `showFocusBar` (same behavior as today's `renderContainer={true}` path)
+- [ ] A single `[data-focused]` CSS rule paints the ring — no per-consumer ring class
+- [ ] `FocusHighlight` is either deleted or reduced to an inert DOM container (no `data-focused` / scroll logic inside)
+- [ ] `spatial-grid-fixture.tsx`'s `FixtureCellDiv` no longer manually writes `data-focused` — attribute appears via `FocusScope`
+- [ ] Existing tests green: `focus-scope.test.tsx`, `spatial-nav-canonical.test.tsx`, `spatial-nav-{board,grid,inspector,leftnav,perspective}.test.tsx`, `perspective-tab-bar.test.tsx`, `left-nav.test.tsx` (if present)
+- [ ] Manual smoke: click a grid cell → ring shows. Click a board card → ring shows. Open inspector, press `j` → ring moves between fields. Press `h` to reach LeftNav → ring shows on view button. Press `k` to reach perspective bar → ring shows on tab. Row selector focused → ring shows. In all cases, the ring is visually identical (one style, one place).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.test.tsx` block: `describe("renderContainer=false data-focused propagation")` — render a `FocusScope` with `renderContainer={false}` whose child reads `useFocusScopeElementRef()` and attaches to a `<div>`; focus the scope via `setFocus(moniker)`; assert the `<div>` gains `data-focused="true"`; unclaim; assert it's gone.
+- [ ] New `focus-scope.test.tsx` block: `it("showFocusBar=false skips the data-focused write")` — identical setup but `showFocusBar={false}` on the `FocusScope`; assert the `<div>` never gets the attribute.
+- [ ] New `focus-scope.test.tsx` block: `it("claimed scope scrolls its element into view")` — stub `HTMLElement.prototype.scrollIntoView`, claim a `renderContainer={false}` scope, assert the stub was called once with `{ block: "nearest" }`. This is a regression guard for the scroll behavior migrating from `FocusHighlight`.
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- focus-scope` — all three new tests pass
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav` — entire spatial-nav-*.test.tsx suite stays green (sanity check that automatic `data-focused` on the scope element satisfies every existing assertion)
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` — full UI test suite green
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the three new focus-scope tests first (failing because `FocusScope` doesn't currently set `data-focused` on `renderContainer={false}` scopes). Add the imperative writer. Delete or simplify `FocusHighlight`. Move the CSS rule. Remove `FixtureCellDiv`'s manual write. Run the full suite.
+- On completion, update tasks `01KPQX6TEZG9SG88B31KGKS2D5`, `01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP`, `01KPQY69TH76VN330BH2J24YSX` — the visual portion is resolved; the LeftNav Enter-binding portion of `01KPQXEMJEGVY7JF9HM5JSWTAP` is still open. Close the first and third; trim the LeftNav task to only cover Enter activation.
\ No newline at end of file
